---
title: "GPU rig reverse SSH tunnel setup"
output: github_document
params:
  action: "install"
  server: "root@88.99.96.102"
  port: 2222
---

This executable R Markdown document installs and manages the reverse SSH tunnel
from the gaming rig's WSL instance to the development server. Run it inside WSL
as the normal `sounkoutoure` user, not from a root shell.

Valid actions are `install`, `status`, `restart`, and `uninstall`.

## Run the document

Copy this file to the rig, open a local WSL terminal, change to the directory
containing the file, and run:

```bash
Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render(
  "rig-tunnel-setup.Rmd",
  params = list(action = "install")
)'
```

Do not perform the initial installation through `ssh rig`: replacing a manual
tunnel can terminate the SSH connection that is running the installer. Later
management actions can be selected by changing `"install"` to `"status"`,
`"restart"`, or `"uninstall"`.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
action <- as.character(params$action)
server <- as.character(params$server)
port <- as.integer(params$port)

stopifnot(
  length(action) == 1L,
  action %in% c("install", "status", "restart", "uninstall"),
  length(server) == 1L,
  nzchar(server),
  length(port) == 1L,
  !is.na(port),
  port >= 1024L,
  port <= 65535L
)

Sys.setenv(
  RIG_TUNNEL_ACTION = action,
  RIG_TUNNEL_SERVER = server,
  RIG_TUNNEL_PORT = as.character(port)
)
```

## Execute tunnel management

The following chunk will request `sudo` access. During the first installation,
it creates a dedicated `~/.ssh/rig_tunnel` key if needed and installs that key
on the development server.

```{bash manage-tunnel}
set -euo pipefail

ACTION=${RIG_TUNNEL_ACTION:?}
SERVER=${RIG_TUNNEL_SERVER:?}
PORT=${RIG_TUNNEL_PORT:?}
INSTALLER="$HOME/rig_tunnel_daemon.sh"
KEY="$HOME/.ssh/rig_tunnel"

if [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: render this Rmd as the normal WSL user, not root." >&2
    exit 1
fi

sudo -v

if [ "$ACTION" = install ]; then
    if [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" != systemd ]; then
        cat >&2 <<'EOF'
ERROR: systemd is not PID 1.

Add this to /etc/wsl.conf, preserving existing settings:

[boot]
systemd=true

Then run `wsl --shutdown` from Windows PowerShell, reopen WSL, and rerun this
Rmd file.
EOF
        exit 1
    fi

    install -d -m 700 "$HOME/.ssh"
    if [ ! -f "$KEY" ]; then
        ssh-keygen \
            -t ed25519 \
            -f "$KEY" \
            -N '' \
            -C 'rig reverse tunnel'
    fi

    if ! ssh \
        -i "$KEY" \
        -o IdentitiesOnly=yes \
        -o BatchMode=yes \
        -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new \
        -o ConnectTimeout=10 \
        "$SERVER" true
    then
        echo "Installing the dedicated tunnel key on $SERVER"
        ssh-copy-id -i "$KEY.pub" "$SERVER"
    fi

    ssh \
        -i "$KEY" \
        -o IdentitiesOnly=yes \
        -o BatchMode=yes \
        -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes \
        -o ConnectTimeout=10 \
        "$SERVER" true
fi

cat > "$INSTALLER" <<'SCRIPT'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Install and supervise the reverse SSH tunnel from the rig's WSL instance to
# port 2222 on the development server.
set -euo pipefail

SERVER=${SERVER:-root@88.99.96.102}
PORT=${PORT:-2222}
UNIT=/etc/systemd/system/rig-tunnel.service
ACTION=${1:-install}

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
    exec sudo --preserve-env=SERVER,PORT bash "$0" "$@"
fi

TUNNEL_USER=${SUDO_USER:-}
if [ -z "$TUNNEL_USER" ] || [ "$TUNNEL_USER" = root ]; then
    echo "ERROR: run this script as the normal WSL user, without a leading sudo." >&2
    echo "Example: bash $0" >&2
    exit 1
fi

TUNNEL_HOME=$(getent passwd "$TUNNEL_USER" | cut -d: -f6)
if [ -z "$TUNNEL_HOME" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: cannot find the home directory for $TUNNEL_USER" >&2
    exit 1
fi

KEY="$TUNNEL_HOME/.ssh/rig_tunnel"

case "$ACTION" in
status)
    systemctl status rig-tunnel.service --no-pager -l || true
    echo
    echo "== last 30 log lines =="
    journalctl -u rig-tunnel.service -n 30 --no-pager || true
    exit 0
    ;;
restart)
    systemctl restart rig-tunnel.service
    systemctl status rig-tunnel.service --no-pager -l
    exit 0
    ;;
uninstall)
    systemctl disable --now rig-tunnel.service 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$UNIT"
    systemctl daemon-reload
    echo "rig-tunnel.service removed"
    exit 0
    ;;
install)
    ;;
*)
    echo "usage: $0 [install|status|restart|uninstall]" >&2
    exit 2
    ;;
esac

if [ "$(ps -p 1 -o comm=)" != systemd ]; then
    echo "ERROR: systemd is not PID 1 in this WSL distribution" >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$KEY" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: missing dedicated SSH key: $KEY" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Verify authentication before installing a service restart loop.
sudo -u "$TUNNEL_USER" -H /usr/bin/ssh \
    -i "$KEY" \
    -o IdentitiesOnly=yes \
    -o BatchMode=yes \
    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes \
    -o ConnectTimeout=10 \
    "$SERVER" true

# The reverse tunnel forwards to this WSL instance's sshd.
if [ ! -x /usr/sbin/sshd ]; then
    apt-get update -qq
    apt-get install -y -qq openssh-server
fi

systemctl enable --now ssh.service
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet ssh.service; then
    echo "ERROR: local ssh.service did not start" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Stop the previous managed service and any hand-started tunnel.
systemctl stop rig-tunnel.service 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -u "$TUNNEL_USER" -f "[a]utossh.*-R .*${PORT}.*:22" 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -u "$TUNNEL_USER" -f "[s]sh.*-R .*${PORT}.*:22" 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

cat > "$UNIT" <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=Reverse SSH tunnel from GPU rig WSL to development server
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target ssh.service
Requires=ssh.service
StartLimitIntervalSec=0

[Service]
Type=exec
User=${TUNNEL_USER}
Environment=HOME=${TUNNEL_HOME}
ExecStart=/usr/bin/ssh -NT \\
    -i ${KEY} \\
    -o IdentitiesOnly=yes \\
    -o BatchMode=yes \\
    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes \\
    -o ExitOnForwardFailure=yes \\
    -o ConnectTimeout=10 \\
    -o ServerAliveInterval=30 \\
    -o ServerAliveCountMax=3 \\
    -R 127.0.0.1:${PORT}:127.0.0.1:22 \\
    ${SERVER}
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
TimeoutStopSec=10
NoNewPrivileges=yes

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now rig-tunnel.service
sleep 3

if ! systemctl is-active --quiet rig-tunnel.service; then
    echo "ERROR: rig-tunnel.service failed" >&2
    journalctl -u rig-tunnel.service -n 30 --no-pager >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "rig-tunnel.service is active"
echo "Logs: journalctl -u rig-tunnel.service -f"
SCRIPT

chmod 700 "$INSTALLER"
SERVER="$SERVER" PORT="$PORT" bash "$INSTALLER" "$ACTION"
```

## Windows logon startup

Systemd starts the service whenever this WSL distribution boots. Windows must
also start the distribution after login. In an Administrator PowerShell, first
obtain the exact distribution name:

```powershell
wsl -l -q
```

Then replace `Ubuntu-24.04` below if necessary and run:

```powershell
$Distro = "Ubuntu-24.04"
schtasks.exe /Create /TN "WSL rig tunnel" /SC ONLOGON /RL HIGHEST /F /TR "C:\Windows\System32\wsl.exe -d $Distro -u root -e /bin/true"
```

Test the scheduled task:

```powershell
schtasks.exe /Run /TN "WSL rig tunnel"
wsl.exe -d $Distro -- systemctl status rig-tunnel.service --no-pager
```

## Development-server verification

Run this on the development server after the installation succeeds:

```bash
ssh rig 'hostname; /usr/lib/wsl/lib/nvidia-smi'
```
